items.check: Define landmark region for the cover title area
items.example: Landmark: <header> — Contains the product logo lockup and 'Annotation Toolkit' title. Marks the top-level branding region of the page/document.

items.check: Define landmark region for the annotations showcase area
items.example: Landmark: <main> — Contains the rotated annotation examples frame (Keys, System Feedback, Button Success, etc.). This is the primary content area of the cover.

items.check: Assign heading level to 'Annotation Toolkit' title text
items.example: Heading Level: H1 — Text: 'Annotation Toolkit'. This is the primary title of the cover and should be the single H1 on this page/screen.

items.check: Confirm no additional headings exist within the background or annotation frames that need hierarchy assignment
items.example: Note: No H2–H6 headings are expected on this cover. If any annotation category labels (e.g. 'Keys', 'Form', 'Table') are exposed as text, they do NOT require heading markup — they are decorative illustration elements.

items.check: Determine whether the Logo Lockup graphic is decorative or informative and annotate accordingly
items.example: Image role: Decorative — Logo Lockup inside Title frame. Alt text: empty (alt="") because the adjacent text node 'Annotation Toolkit' already conveys the identity. Mark as aria-hidden="true".

items.check: Annotate the Background / BG frame and Background mist rectangle as decorative
items.example: Image role: Decorative — Background, BG frame, Background mist rectangle, and Git lines group are purely visual/aesthetic. Mark all as aria-hidden="true". No alt text required.

items.check: Annotate the Annotations illustration frame (rotated -47deg) as decorative
items.example: Image role: Decorative — The 'Annotations (Editable)' frame containing Keys, System Feedback, Button Success, Reading Warning, Arrow Stops, Focus, Heading, Preset, List, Form, Note, Table, Landmarks, Meta sub-frames is a visual design showcase. Mark entire group as aria-hidden="true". No alt text required.

items.check: Annotate the Annotations rectangle overlay as decorative
items.example: Image role: Decorative — 'Annotations' rectangle is a visual overlay element. Mark as aria-hidden="true".

items.check: Define the page/document title for this cover screen
items.example: Page Title: 'Annotation Toolkit — GitHub Annotation Toolkit Cover'. Ensure the HTML <title> or document title attribute reflects the product name shown on the cover.

items.check: Specify the language of the cover content
items.example: Language: lang="en" — Set on the root <html> element (or equivalent document root). All visible text on this cover is English.

items.check: Verify colour contrast of 'Annotation Toolkit' title text against the Background mist / BG frame
items.example: Concern: Confirm that the 'Annotation Toolkit' title text meets a minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio against whatever background colour or mist overlay sits behind it. (WCAG 1.4.3 AA). Check both light and dark background states if applicable.

items.check: Confirm Logo Lockup has sufficient contrast or is supplemented by text
items.example: Concern: If the Logo Lockup contains meaningful graphical elements (e.g. icon, wordmark strokes), ensure non-text contrast of 3:1 against adjacent background colours (WCAG 1.4.11). If purely decorative, this can be skipped.

items.check: Note that the rotated annotations frame is intended as illustration only
items.example: Designer Note: The 'Annotations (Editable: rotate -47deg to reorganize)' frame is a decorative showcase of annotation category examples. It should be hidden from assistive technologies in production (aria-hidden="true") and is not intended to be interactive or readable by screen readers.

items.check: Document the design status of the Cover canvas component
items.example: Design Status: Cover — This is a title/splash canvas frame for the GitHub Annotation Toolkit design file. It is not a shipped UI component; no production implementation is expected. Accessibility annotations applied here serve as reference examples for the toolkit itself.

items.check: Note the editorial rotation of the Annotations frame for design organisation
items.example: Canvas Note: The 'Annotations (Editable)' frame is rotated -47deg for visual layout purposes on the cover. This is a design-time property only and has no bearing on the DOM order or accessibility tree in any implemented version.
